file_name: Attachment B - Intent to Propose and Non-Disclosure Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Section 2. Preservation of Confidentiality . All Confidential Information (a) supplied by any employee, agent, consultant, or independent contractor of State ("State Representatives ") to the Recipient or any employee, agent, officer, director, shareholder, independent contractor or representativ e of the Recipient (collectively, the "Recipient Representatives "), (b) obtained by the Recipient or any Recipient Representatives from any documents, meetings or telephone conversations with any State Representatives or from books or records of State , (c) obtained by the Recipient or any Recipient Representatives or in any other manner including through hosting the software evaluation on Recipient 's website, or (d) jointly or individually developed by State and/or Recipient shall be protected and main tained by the Recipient on a confidential basis and the Recipient shall not use any of the Confidential Information for any purposes (other than as permitted by this Agreement). The Recipient shall refrain from directly or indirectly disclosing any of the Confidential Information to any person, firm, fund, or entity, or knowingly making any Confidential Information available to any others for any use (other than as permitted by this Agreement). Failure to mark any of the Confidential Information as 3 confid ential or proprietary shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2. Preservation of Confidentiality . All Confidential Information (a) supplied by any employee, agent, consultant, or independent contractor of State ("State Representatives ") to the Recipient or any employee, agent, officer, director, shareholder, independent contractor or representativ e of the Recipient (collectively, the "Recipient Representatives "), (b) obtained by the Recipient or any Recipient Representatives from any documents, meetings or telephone conversations with any State Representatives or from books or records of State , (c) obtained by the Recipient or any Recipient Representatives or in any other manner including through hosting the software evaluation on Recipient 's website, or (d) jointly or individually developed by State and/or Recipient shall be protected and main tained by the Recipient on a confidential basis and the Recipient may use the Confidential Information for the purposes of improving their internal business processes even if unrelated to this agreement. The Recipient shall refrain from directly or indirectly disclosing any of the Confidential Information to any person, firm, fund, or entity, or knowingly making any Confidential Information available to any others for any use (other than as permitted by this Agreement). Failure to mark any of the Confidential Information as 3 confid ential or proprietary shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds that "Recipient may use the Confidential Information for the purposes of improving their internal business processes even if unrelated to this agreement." This directly contradicts the statement in the same section that "the Recipient shall not use any of the Confidential Information for any purposes (other than as permitted by this Agreement)." This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2. Preservation of Confidentiality . All Confidential Information (a) supplied by any employee, agent, consultant, or independent contractor of State ("State Representatives ") to the Recipient or any employee, agent, officer, director, shareholder, independent contractor or representativ e of the Recipient (collectively, the "Recipient Representatives "), (b) obtained by the Recipient or any Recipient Representatives from any documents, meetings or telephone conversations with any State Representatives or from books or records of State , (c) obtained by the Recipient or any Recipient Representatives or in any other manner including through hosting the software evaluation on Recipient 's website, or (d) jointly or individually developed by State and/or Recipient shall be protected and main tained by the Recipient on a confidential basis and the Recipient may use the Confidential Information for the purposes of improving their internal business processes even if unrelated to this agreement. The Recipient shall refrain from directly or indirectly disclosing any of the Confidential Information to any person, firm, fund, or entity, or knowingly making any Confidential Information available to any others for any use (other than as permitted by this Agreement). Failure to mark any of the Confidential Information as 3 confid ential or proprietary shall not affect its status as Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds that the Recipient may use Confidential Information to improve internal business processes unrelated to the agreement, which directly contradicts the original text's restriction that the Recipient shall not use Confidential Information for any purposes other than as permitted by this Agreement. The contradicted text is actually the changed text itself, where this contradiction exists in Section 2. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition as a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Section 7. Fulfillment of Purpose . When requested by State , the Recipient shall: (a) Return all documents, copies of do cuments, computer records and other means of recording or storing Confidential Information in Recipient 's or Recipient Representatives ' possession to State within five (5) calendar days or, at Recipient 's option, destroy all such items; (b) Certify in writing to State that the Recipient has so complied; and (c) Not use or disclose the Confidential Information or transact business in any manner based upon the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 7. Fulfillment of Purpose . When requested by State , the Recipient shall: (a) Return all documents, copies of do cuments, computer records and other means of recording or storing Confidential Information in Recipient 's or Recipient Representatives ' possession to State within five (5) calendar days; (b) Certify in writing to State that the Recipient has so complied; and (c) Not use or disclose the Confidential Information or transact business in any manner based upon the Confidential Information. Recipient can use, or destroy the mentioned documents at its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This adds the sentence "Recipient can use, or destroy the mentioned documents at its discretion." which contradicts the prior statement that the recipient shall return all documents when requested by State. It creates an in-text contradiction in Section 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 7. Fulfillment of Purpose . When requested by State , the Recipient shall: (a) Return all documents, copies of do cuments, computer records and other means of recording or storing Confidential Information in Recipient 's or Recipient Representatives ' possession to State within five (5) calendar days; (b) Certify in writing to State that the Recipient has so complied; and (c) Not use or disclose the Confidential Information or transact business in any manner based upon the Confidential Information. Recipient can use, or destroy the mentioned documents at its discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'Recipient can use, or destroy the mentioned documents at its discretion' to Section 7. This directly contradicts the initial requirement in the same section that 'Recipient shall: (a) Return all documents... when requested by State'. The recipient is now given the option to not return documents that they were previously obligated to return, creating a clear and direct conflict within Section 7, thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3. Report of Breach. Recipient shall promptly notify State of a breach of any Confidential Information within forty -eight (48) hours of when Recipient discovers such breach. A breach shall be treated as discovered by Recipient as of the first day on which such breach is known, or by exercising reasonable diligence would have been known, to any person, othe r than the person committing the breach, who is an employee, officer, Recipient Representative or other agent of Recipient. Recipient' s notification shall be in writing and , if the Confidential Information is PHI or PII , shall include identification of each individual whose PHI or PII has been, or is reasonably believed by Recipient to have been , subject to the breach.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3. Report of Breach. Recipient shall promptly notify State of a breach of any Confidential Information within forty -eight (48) hours of when Recipient discovers such breach. A breach shall be treated as discovered by Recipient as of the first day on which such breach is known, or by exercising reasonable diligence would have been known, to the head of legal department. Recipient' s notification shall be in writing and , if the Confidential Information is PHI or PII , shall not include identification of each individual whose PHI or PII has been, or is reasonably believed by Recipient to have been , subject to the breach.
perturbation.explanation: The changes contradict established obligations: changing 'any person' to 'head of legal department' narrows the discovery trigger, potentially delaying breach reporting and removing the need to identify individuals affected by PHI/PII breaches directly contradicts previous notification requirements. This creates ambiguity regarding breach reporting obligations within Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3. Report of Breach. Recipient shall promptly notify State of a breach of any Confidential Information within forty -eight (48) hours of when Recipient discovers such breach. A breach shall be treated as discovered by Recipient as of the first day on which such breach is known, or by exercising reasonable diligence would have been known, to the head of legal department. Recipient' s notification shall be in writing and , if the Confidential Information is PHI or PII , shall not include identification of each individual whose PHI or PII has been, or is reasonably believed by Recipient to have been , subject to the breach.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the scope of who's knowledge triggers a breach report to only the 'head of legal department' where the original text stated 'any person'. Additionally, the changed text says that if Confidential Information is PHI or PII, notification 'shall not include identification of each individual whose PHI or PII has been, or is reasonably believed by Recipient to have been , subject to the breach', whereas the original text implies that the notification would include that. This contradicts the contradicted text because both sections are in the same location, so this new obligation is directly contradictory to the old obligations, creating a structural flaw, which adheres to the definition.

perturbation.original_text: Section 4. Recipient Representatives . Recipient agrees to ensure that any Recipient Representative to whom it provides Confidential Information agrees in writing to the same restrictions and conditions that apply through this Agreement to Recipient with respect to Confidential Information. Such written agreement shall also require the Recipient Repres entative to implement reasonable and appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards that reasonably and appropriately protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the Confidential Information that it receives. Recipient, and not the State, is solely responsible for Recipient Representatives ' uses and disclosures of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 4. Recipient Representatives . Recipient agrees to ensure that any Recipient Representative to whom it provides Confidential Information agrees in writing to the same restrictions and conditions that apply through this Agreement to Recipient with respect to Confidential Information. Such written agreement shall also require the Recipient Repres entative to implement reasonable and appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards that reasonably and appropriately protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the Confidential Information that it receives. Recipient, and also the State, is jointly responsible for Recipient Representatives ' uses and disclosures of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "Recipient, and not the State, is solely responsible for Recipient Representatives ' uses and disclosures of Confidential Information" to "Recipient, and also the State, is jointly responsible for Recipient Representatives ' uses and disclosures of Confidential Information" directly contradicts the original statement, shifting responsibility to the State. This introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4. Recipient Representatives . Recipient agrees to ensure that any Recipient Representative to whom it provides Confidential Information agrees in writing to the same restrictions and conditions that apply through this Agreement to Recipient with respect to Confidential Information. Such written agreement shall also require the Recipient Repres entative to implement reasonable and appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards that reasonably and appropriately protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the Confidential Information that it receives. Recipient, and also the State, is jointly responsible for Recipient Representatives ' uses and disclosures of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Recipient, and not the State, is solely responsible for Recipient Representatives ' uses and disclosures of Confidential Information." The changed text modifies this to "Recipient, and also the State, is jointly responsible for Recipient Representatives ' uses and disclosures of Confidential Information." This introduces a direct contradiction regarding responsibility for the uses and disclosures of Confidential Information by Recipient Representatives. The changed text explicitly makes the State jointly responsible, which conflicts with the original statement that the State is not responsible, thus creating an in-text contradiction within Section 4. This contradiction is also present when comparing the changed text with the contradicted text since they are one and the same.

perturbation.original_text: Section 9. Indemnification of State . The Recipient shall indemnify and hold State harmless from and against all liability, loss, cost or expense (including attorneys ' fees) which State may sustain or incur by reason of the breach of any agreements, representations, warranties or covenants of or relating to Recipient or any of the Recip ient Representatives contained in this Agreement or by reason of the enforcement by State of any such agreements, representations, warranties or covenants set forth herein. Without limiting the foregoing, in the event of a breach of PHI or PII or similar breach or wrongful disclosure as defined by an applicable law or regulation requiring notification or other remedial action due to the breach or wrongful disclosure of PHI , PII, or other personal or financial information ( "Breach Law") that arose out of or related to Recipient 's or Recipient Representatives ' acts and omissions, Recipient shall indemnify State against all costs and expenses incurred by State that are associated with complying with the notification requirements under the Breach Law. Such indemnification shall include all costs relate d to notifying individuals or any other entity required to be notified by Breach Law, any remediat ion necessitat ed by the b reach, any fines or penalties arising out of the b reach, and any other actions required or that are customary in the industry to be taken pursuant to the Breach Law (including, without limitation, credit or identity monitoring for affected i ndiv iduals).
perturbation.changed_text: Section 9. Indemnification of State . The Recipient shall indemnify and hold State harmless from and against all liability, loss, cost or expense (including attorneys ' fees) which State may sustain or incur by reason of the breach of any agreements, representations, warranties or covenants of or relating to Recipient or any of the Recip ient Representatives contained in this Agreement or by reason of the enforcement by State of any such agreements, representations, warranties or covenants set forth herein. Without limiting the foregoing, in the event of a breach of PHI or PII or similar breach or wrongful disclosure as defined by an applicable law or regulation requiring notification or other remedial action due to the breach or wrongful disclosure of PHI , PII, or other personal or financial information ( "Breach Law") that arose out of or related to Recipient 's or Recipient Representatives ' acts and omissions, Recipient shall only indemnify State against direct costs and expenses incurred by State that are associated with complying with the notification requirements under the Breach Law, in the event that the breach or disclosure was intentional. Such indemnification shall include costs solely related to credit or identity monitoring for affected i ndiv iduals).
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the scope of indemnification, previously applying to all breaches, to only intentional breaches, and reduces the scope to solely cover credit/identity monitoring. It introduces a condition and narrows the scope, creating a potential conflict with the original broad indemnification clause within Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9. Indemnification of State . The Recipient shall indemnify and hold State harmless from and against all liability, loss, cost or expense (including attorneys ' fees) which State may sustain or incur by reason of the breach of any agreements, representations, warranties or covenants of or relating to Recipient or any of the Recip ient Representatives contained in this Agreement or by reason of the enforcement by State of any such agreements, representations, warranties or covenants set forth herein. Without limiting the foregoing, in the event of a breach of PHI or PII or similar breach or wrongful disclosure as defined by an applicable law or regulation requiring notification or other remedial action due to the breach or wrongful disclosure of PHI , PII, or other personal or financial information ( "Breach Law") that arose out of or related to Recipient 's or Recipient Representatives ' acts and omissions, Recipient shall only indemnify State against direct costs and expenses incurred by State that are associated with complying with the notification requirements under the Breach Law, in the event that the breach or disclosure was intentional. Such indemnification shall include costs solely related to credit or identity monitoring for affected i ndiv iduals).
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the scope of indemnification compared to the original text in Section 9. Initially, the Recipient indemnified the State against all liability, loss, cost, or expense. However, the changed text limits this indemnification to only 'direct costs and expenses' and only in the event of 'intentional' breaches, further restricting it to costs related solely to 'credit or identity monitoring.' Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, this directly contradicts the original broad indemnification promise, creating a clear conflict and inconsistency within the section as the first sentence does not include the limitations that the rest of the paragraph now has.

perturbation.original_text: Section 11. Term . Th is Agreement may be terminated by State on five (5) days prior written notice to the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 11. Term . Th is Agreement may be terminated by State on five (5) days prior written notice to the Recipient. This agreement can only be terminated if both parties agree in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This addition contradicts the original statement about termination. The original text states the agreement may be terminated by the state on five day notice. the added text makes the agreement not terminated without both parties written approval. It creates an in-text contradiction in Section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 11. Term . Th is Agreement may be terminated by State on five (5) days prior written notice to the Recipient. This agreement can only be terminated if both parties agree in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'This agreement can only be terminated if both parties agree in writing' to Section 11. This directly contradicts the original statement in the same section that 'This Agreement may be terminated by State on five (5) days prior written notice to the Recipient.' The state's ability to terminate with notice is now negated by the requirement of mutual agreement, creating a clear in-text contradiction and a structural flaw as defined in the instructions.
